toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. To do this with `dotnet test`, I need to use the `--crashdump` option (or the equivalent blame hang/crash options).

With the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true), the crash dump collection is done via `--crashdump` flag.

For the older VSTest-based approach, you'd use `--blame-crash`. Since this project has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, it uses the new Microsoft Testing Platform.

Let me check which options are available. With the Microsoft Testing Platform, the flag is `--crashdump` to collect crash dumps.


Now I'll execute the test command with the crash dump collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c8e297e6402b4d37ba67c11b8f8a822c && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag isn't recognized. This project has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, so it should support Microsoft Testing Platform options. But the error suggests MSBuild is handling it. Let me try the VSTest blame-crash approach instead, or pass the option differently.

With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, `dotnet test` should forward options to the test platform. But the `--crashdump` option might need to be passed differently - perhaps with `--` separator.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the `--crashdump` is a Microsoft Testing Platform option. But with `dotnet test` integration, the options need to be passed after `--`. Let me try that.
 
I'm second-guessing myself on the syntax here. The `--blame-crash` approach is the VSTest way, but since this uses the new Microsoft Testing Platform through `dotnet test`, I should stick with `--crashdump` and make sure it's passed correctly with the `--` separator to forward it to the platform.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command to run your tests with crash-dump collection:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Since this project uses the **Microsoft Testing Platform** (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`), the `--crashdump` option is passed after the `--` separator. If any test crashes the host process, a dump file will be written to the test results directory for post-mortem analysis.
